runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 4 in a U.S. microsimulation model, measuring the response coefficient to the bounded relative change in the effective marginal wage for primary earners in that decile.
citations: General labor-supply elasticity estimates for phase-in/phase-out tax-benefit margins in the literature

runs.reasoningSummary: I expect a modest positive elasticity in decile 4—larger than near-zero due to income-tax and benefit participation/adjustment margins, but well below high values—reflecting limited behavioral flexibility and averaging across multiple mechanisms within a bounded-marginal-wage tax-benefit simulation.
